title: knema kunstleri subsp. macrophylla
intro: Knema kunstleri subsp. macrophylla, also known as the Borneo nut or wild nut, is a subspecies of plant that belongs to the family Myristicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of Knema kunstleri subsp. macrophylla include reducing fever, treating skin infections, and alleviating gastrointestinal disorders. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's seeds and leaves are often used in traditional remedies, typically dried and mixed with other herbs to create teas, infusions, or tonics.
